# Data Processing Agreement (Template)

> **Read this first:** like the [`MasterServiceAgreement.md`](MasterServiceAgreement.md), this is a starting draft, not a vetted legal contract. A DPA governs real regulatory compliance (UK GDPR) — have a qualified solicitor review before using it with an actual client, especially one in a regulated sector (health, finance).

Used when a project involves Aegis processing the **client's own customers' personal data** on the client's behalf — e.g. building a booking system that stores the client's customers' names/emails, not just Aegis's own enquiry data (which is covered by the site's [Privacy Policy](/privacy-policy.html) and [Data Processing Notice](/data-processing-notice.html) instead).

This Data Processing Agreement ("DPA") is entered into between **[Client Legal Name]** ("Controller") and **Aegis Health AI Ltd**, Company No. 16695420 ("Processor"), and supplements the Master Service Agreement between the parties.

## 1. Subject matter and duration
Processor processes personal data on Controller's behalf only for the duration of the relevant Statement of Work, and for any agreed Managed Services period afterward.

## 2. Nature and purpose of processing
[Describe specifically — e.g. "storage and display of customer booking records (name, email, phone, appointment time) within the booking system built under SOW #___"]

## 3. Categories of data subjects and personal data
**Data subjects:** [e.g. Controller's customers]
**Data:** [e.g. name, email, phone, booking history — list only what's actually collected]

## 4. Processor's obligations
Processor will:
- Process personal data only on Controller's documented instructions.
- Ensure anyone processing the data (i.e. Aegis and any subcontractor) is bound by confidentiality.
- Implement appropriate technical and organisational security measures (see [`SecurityPolicy.md`](SecurityPolicy.md)).
- Assist Controller in responding to data subject rights requests.
- Notify Controller without undue delay on becoming aware of a personal data breach (see [`IncidentResponsePlan.md`](IncidentResponsePlan.md)).
- Delete or return all personal data at the end of the engagement, at Controller's choice, unless retention is required by law.

## 5. Sub-processors
Processor uses the following sub-processors, each bound by its own data protection terms: Vercel (hosting), and any project-specific third party disclosed in the relevant SOW. Processor will notify Controller before adding a new sub-processor with access to Controller's data.

## 6. International transfers
[State explicitly if any sub-processor stores/processes data outside the UK/EEA, and what safeguard applies — e.g. Vercel's standard contractual clauses. Confirm the actual current facts before using this clause with a real client.]

## 7. Audit
Controller may request reasonable evidence of Processor's compliance with this DPA, no more than once per 12 months absent a specific concern.
